target.title: fix(browser): document stable tab references
target.body:
## Summary

- Makes browser tab references explicit across the CLI, tool schema, docs, and browser skill: callers should prefer `suggestedTargetId`, labels, or `tabId`, while raw `targetId` and unique raw prefixes remain compatible.
- Explains why stable tab references matter for agents: `tabId`/labels are shorter than raw CDP target ids, cheaper to keep in prompts, easier for humans and models to read, less error-prone to copy across turns, and more durable when Chromium replaces the underlying raw target.
- Adds contract coverage for friendly tab reference resolution, alias migration across raw target replacement, route-level focus, CLI output, and schema wording.
- Centralizes repeated CLI tab-reference help text so command help and future wording updates do not drift.

## Why this matters

Raw CDP target ids are implementation handles. They are long, visually opaque, and can change when Chromium replaces a page target after navigation, form submission, or provider-specific target handling. Agents often need to carry a tab handle across multiple turns; a short `tabId` such as `t5` or a label such as `checkout` costs fewer tokens, is easier to verify in logs, and reduces the chance of copying the wrong long id.

OpenClaw already supported these friendlier handles in the resolver. This PR makes that contract visible and test-covered without breaking existing callers: the field remains named `targetId`, raw target ids and unique raw prefixes still work, and the backend continues to receive the raw target id after resolution.

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-manage.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/target-id.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.tab-selection-state.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/tabs.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.schema.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check -- extensions/browser docs`
- `node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base upstream/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Browser tab APIs already accepted friendly tab references, but CLI help, schema descriptions, docs, and skill guidance still presented raw CDP target ids as the primary handle. This change makes the stable handle contract explicit while keeping existing raw `targetId` inputs compatible.

Real environment tested: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64, Node v24.16.0, dev profile gateway running from source with `pnpm dev --profile dev gateway run`, managed Chromium through the browser plugin. Head verified locally: `118af80b0b0ae4e5cc1069bcf1ff8649f135cc86`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the dev gateway from this branch, opened `https://example.com` with `--label rebaseproof`, listed tabs, focused the tab by label, focused the same tab by returned `tabId`, captured an ARIA snapshot with `--target-id rebaseproof`, then closed it by label.

Evidence after fix: `openclaw browser tabs` printed `1. Example Domain [use: rebaseproof tab: t1 label:rebaseproof] ... id: <raw target id>`. `openclaw browser focus rebaseproof` and `openclaw browser focus t1` both succeeded. `openclaw browser --json snapshot --target-id rebaseproof --format aria --limit 5` returned `ok: true`, `url: https://example.com/`, and a heading node named `Example Domain`.

Observed result after fix: The same real browser tab could be addressed by the recommended `suggestedTargetId` label and by its `tabId`; the backend response still returned the raw `targetId`, preserving compatibility for existing callers.

What was not tested: Existing-session / Chrome MCP profile parity was not exercised in the live run. The touched resolver and tab selection tests cover the shared friendly-handle contract; existing-session action capability limits are unchanged.
target.author: fmls
